zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱，短期承压整理
zh.content:
当前AVAX价格围绕6.61附近窄幅震荡，整体运行在SMA20（6.79）下方，说明中期仍处弱势修复阶段。MACD维持负值，空头动能略占优势。短线缺乏明确方向  

均线压制明显，价格在6.78下方反复受阻，上方抛压较重。MACD绿柱缩短，动能走弱但未见反转  

支撑6.59/6.55，失守或下探6.45；阻力6.70与6.78，突破后改善区间空间有限  

短线建议区间操作，6.70未突破前不追多，反弹阻力附近可分批减仓，控制风险

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is currently showing a mild bearish bias with price trading slightly below the short-term SMA(20) at 6.78663. The market structure reflects a short-term downtrend or consolidation phase after failed attempts to reclaim higher levels. Price action near 6.615 sits close to the intraday low range, indicating limited bullish momentum. Overall sentiment remains cautious as buyers struggle to regain control above the 6.70 region.

The MACD reading at -0.0586 confirms weakening momentum and sustained bearish pressure, suggesting sellers are still dominant in the short term. With RSI data unavailable, momentum assessment leans heavily on price positioning relative to the SMA(20), reinforcing downside bias. The SMA(20) is now acting as dynamic resistance, and repeated rejection beneath it highlights ongoing distribution rather than accumulation. Any recovery attempts currently lack strong confirmation signals.

Immediate support is positioned around 6.60, closely aligned with recent intraday lows at 6.597. A breakdown below this level could open the door toward 6.55 and potentially 6.45 if selling pressure accelerates. On the upside, initial resistance stands at 6.63, followed by stronger resistance at the SMA(20) near 6.79 and a psychological barrier around 7.00. This creates a compressed range where price is likely to react sharply at boundaries.

From a trading perspective, the environment favors cautious, range-based strategies rather than aggressive trend following. Short setups may be considered on rejection near 6.63–6.70 with tight risk control, while invalidation should be placed above 6.80 to avoid breakout traps. Long positions are better reserved for a confirmed reclaim of the SMA(20) with improving momentum. Until then, disciplined scalping between support and resistance remains the more structured approach.
